messages.content: SizedBox flutter use child length

As an AI language model, I don't understand the context of your question. However, in general, we cannot use the child length directly in a SizedBox. We use the SizedBox widget to give a fixed dimension to a child widget. The size is provided using the height and width properties of the SizedBox widget. We can assign these properties with either static values or dynamic values. But, we cannot directly use the child widget’s length as the height and width properties. To use dynamic values, we must use MediaQuery to provide contextual values to the SizedBox widget.
appbar title align left

To align the title of an AppBar to the left, you can use the `AppBar`'s `title` property and set it to a `Row` widget with `MainAxisAlignment.start` alignment.

Here's an example code snippet that shows how to align the title of an AppBar to the left:

```dart
AppBar(
  title: Row(
    mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.start,
    children: [
      Text('Your Title Here'),
    ],
  ),
);
```

In the above code, we set the `title` property of the `AppBar` to a `Row` widget with `MainAxisAlignment.start` alignment. This aligns the title to the left of the app bar. The `Text` widget contains the title text that we want to display.

You can customize the style of the title text by passing additional properties to the `Text` widget, like `style`, `fontSize`, `fontWeight`, `color`, and more.
tabbar tabs align left
tabbar flutter no space in tabs

If you want to remove the space between the tabs in a `TabBar` widget in Flutter, you can set the `indicatorPadding` property of the `TabBar` to `EdgeInsets.zero`.

Here's an example code snippet that shows how to remove the space between the tabs in a `TabBar`:

```dart
TabBar(
  indicatorPadding: EdgeInsets.zero,
  tabs: <Widget>[
    Tab(
      text: 'Tab 1',
    ),
    Tab(
      text: 'Tab 2',
    ),
    ...
  ],
);
```

In the above code, we set the `indicatorPadding` property of the `TabBar` to `EdgeInsets.zero`. This removes the space between the tabs in the `TabBar`.

You can also customize the style and behavior of the `TabBar` by passing additional properties to the constructor, like `isScrollable`, `controller`, `indicatorColor`, `labelColor`, `unselectedLabelColor`, and more.
flutter add bottom line for appbar align with indicator

If you want to add a bottom line to the `AppBar` in Flutter that aligns with the indicator of a `TabBar`, you can use the `PreferredSize` and `bottom` properties of the `AppBar` to customize its height and add a `TabBar` widget as the `bottom` property of the `AppBar`.

Here's an example code snippet that shows how to add a bottom line to the `AppBar` that aligns with the indicator of a `TabBar`:

```dart
AppBar(
  elevation: 0,
  backgroundColor: Colors.white,
  bottom: PreferredSize(
    preferredSize: Size.fromHeight(1.0),
    child: Container(
      color: Colors.grey,
      height: 1.0,
      child: TabBar(
        indicatorColor: Colors.blue,
        tabs: [
          Tab(text: 'Tab 1'),
          Tab(text: 'Tab 2'),
        ],
      ),
    ),
  ),
),
```

In the above code, we created an `AppBar` with a custom height of 1.0 using the `PreferredSize` widget and set the `bottom` property of the `AppBar` to a `Container` widget with a height of 1.0 and a grey background color. 

We added a `TabBar` widget as a child of the `Container` and set the `indicatorColor` property of the `TabBar` to blue to match the indicator color of the tabs. This will help align the bottom line with the indicator.

You can customize the style and behavior of the `AppBar` and `TabBar` widgets by passing additional properties to their constructors, like `elevation`, `backgroundColor`, `indicatorColor`, `labelColor`, `unselectedLabelColor`, and more.
